key_themes: Mixed Q1 2026 earnings (EPS beat with 15% YoY growth but revenue/NII miss)
Strong loan growth (11%) and deposit growth (7%) with positive segment momentum
Net interest margin compression and higher efficiency ratio pressuring results
Maintained full-year 2026 NII guidance (~$50B) and robust capital returns (buybacks)
Analyst Moderate Buy consensus with ~15-20% upside to $95–97 price targets despite post-earnings dip
summary: Wells Fargo reported solid underlying progress in Q1 2026 with EPS of $1.60 (beating estimates and up 15% YoY), strong double-digit loan and deposit growth, and continued capital returns, while closing its last major consent order. However, revenue of $21.45B and net interest income missed expectations amid margin compression, prompting a 5–6% share price drop immediately after the April 14 release. Sentiment is mixed-to-neutral in the near term—investors are disappointed by the softer top-line and NIM trends but analysts remain constructive on the growth trajectory, maintained guidance, and valuation upside.[[1]](https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/wells-fargo-profit-rises-interest-income-boost-2026-04-14/)[[2]](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Wells+Fargo+shares+slide+after+Q1+results+come+in+mixed,+with+revenue+and+net+interest+income+missing+expectations)[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/WFC/forecast/)
sources: 15+ news articles and earnings releases (Wells Fargo, Reuters, Seeking Alpha, Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool), Analyst ratings from 20–25 firms showing Moderate Buy/Overweight consensus, ~30–40 relevant X posts analyzed (primarily earnings reaction on/after April 14, 2026)
